# WMA test fixtures — provenance

These fixtures are deterministic, single-pin synthetic streams generated by
ffmpeg from a 1-second 440 Hz sine wave (`sine=f=440:duration=1`).  They are
checked in so the round-59+ ASF/WAVEFORMATEX extractor + DirectShow
`IPin::ReceiveConnection` smoke tests have a stable input that does NOT depend
on a network fetch or a system ffmpeg install.

Both fixtures are mono / 44 100 Hz / 32 kbit/s, sized < 8 KiB.

## Generation command

```bash
ffmpeg -y -f lavfi -i sine=f=440:duration=1 -ac 1 -c:a wmav1 -b:a 32k \
    wma1_440hz_mono_1s.wma
ffmpeg -y -f lavfi -i sine=f=440:duration=1 -ac 1 -c:a wmav2 -b:a 32k \
    wma2_440hz_mono_1s.wma
```

## ffmpeg version used at generation time

- ffmpeg 8.1 (Homebrew, Apple clang 21.0.0)
- libavcodec 62.28.100
- libavformat 62.12.100

## Inspected WAVEFORMATEX (lifted from the Stream Properties Object's
## Type-Specific Data field — see the ASF Specification §3 / §4)

### `wma1_440hz_mono_1s.wma`

- `wFormatTag` = `0x0160` (WAVE_FORMAT_MSAUDIO1)
- `nChannels` = 1
- `nSamplesPerSec` = 44100
- `nAvgBytesPerSec` = 4000  (32 kbit/s)
- `nBlockAlign` = 185
- `wBitsPerSample` = 16
- `cbSize` = 4
- extradata (4 B) = `00 00 01 00`

### `wma2_440hz_mono_1s.wma`

- `wFormatTag` = `0x0161` (WAVE_FORMAT_WMAUDIO2)
- `nChannels` = 1
- `nSamplesPerSec` = 44100
- `nAvgBytesPerSec` = 4000
- `nBlockAlign` = 185
- `wBitsPerSample` = 16
- `cbSize` = 10
- extradata (10 B) = `00 00 00 00 01 00 00 00 00 00`

These are the bytes the splitter's `IPin::QueryAccept` will validate against
expected WMA1/WMA2 header constants when a downstream filter feeds it a
synthetic `AM_MEDIA_TYPE`.  Round 58's all-zero extradata was rejected with
`E_FAIL`; the round-59 extractor pulls these values out of the fixture and
restages them into the `AM_MEDIA_TYPE` blueprint.
